text:

Explain.

subject: english
text:
“There he stands like a black Ancient Mariner.”
A) Who does ‘he’ refer to, here?
B) Why did he stand like the Ancient Mariner?
C) What is the figure of speech employed here?

subject: english
text:
“His rough unshaven face shows pain.”
A) Who said this?
B) Who is the speaker describing here?
C) What does the ‘pain’ refer to?

subject: english
text:
“He was sent here to preach the Black Gospel of Jazz.”
A) Who was sent to preach the Black Gospel ?
B) How did the ‘he’ preach the gospel?
C) What do you mean by ‘gospel’ in this context?

subject: english
text:
“He is no longer a man, but a bird.”
A) Who got transformed into a bird?
B) Why is the speaker saying so?
C) When was the transformation observed?

subject: english
text:
“His run-down shoes have paper in them.”
A) Who is the ‘he’ here?
B) Why does his shoes have papers in them?
C) What does this statement tell about the person spoken about here?

subject: english
text:
“Gently he lifts it now to parted lips, to tell the world that he is a black man.”
A) What does he lift?
B) Why does he lift it?
C) Why does he want to tell the world that he is a black man?

subject: english
text:
“His head still down, eyes still closed, ears perked.”
A) Who is the ‘his’ here?
B) What do you mean by ‘perked’?
C) Why did the speaker make this observation?

subject: english
text:
“Now preaching it with words of screaming notes.”
A) Who is preaching now?
B) What does ‘it’ refer to?
C) Why was the preacher using ‘words of screaming notes’?
